name: web_extract
description: Fetch a URL and extract structured records from it in one step. Prefer this over chaining fetch_html/web_read + extract_records when you start from a URL. Pass `fields=["name","url","rating"]` for the simple case; the runtime builds the JSON Schema for you.

url.description: URL to fetch and extract records from.

fields.description: Simple field list, e.g. ["name", "url", "rating"]. Names like rating/price/count are auto-typed as numbers; everything else is string. Prefer this over `schema` for typical web-page extraction.

schema.description: Advanced: full JSON Schema describing ONE record. Use this only when you need nested objects, arrays, or strict types beyond what `fields` provides. If both `fields` and `schema` are supplied, `schema` wins. If neither is supplied, the tool falls back to a best-effort heuristic extraction (name/url/price/rating/description).

max_pages.description: Follow rel=next or ?page=N up to this cap. Default 1; hard cap 5.

implementation_notes: GET with v0.8.20 polite headers (User-Agent + Accept); detects empty/soft-block bodies and returns degraded shape without an LLM call. Pagination added in T4. LLM-usability fix: `fields=[...]` builds a JSON Schema internally; bare call (no schema/fields) runs a non-LLM heuristic extraction over the most-common repeated container (li/article/section/div/tr) for {name, url, price, rating, description}.
